perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the Distributor wishes to retain S2K, through registered representatives of S2K ("Authorized S2K Representatives"), to introduce the Funds to registered representatives of broker-dealers and registered investment advisers located at the financial institutions (each, an "Intermediary" and collectively, "Intermediaries") that may have customers interested in investing in a Fund.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the Distributor wishes to retain S2K, through registered representatives of S2K ("Authorized S2K Representatives"), to introduce the Funds to registered representatives of broker-dealers and registered investment advisers located at the financial institutions (each, an "Intermediary" and collectively, "Intermediaries") that may have customers interested in investing in a Fund. For the purpose of this agreement, an 'Intermediary' is defined strictly as a broker-dealer registered with the SEC and FINRA.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a restrictive definition to 'Intermediary'. Initially, 'Intermediary' includes both broker-dealers and registered investment advisers. By adding 'For the purpose of this agreement, an 'Intermediary' is defined strictly as a broker-dealer registered with the SEC and FINRA,' it excludes registered investment advisors from being considered intermediaries. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original definition includes registered investment advisers and the updated definition excludes them, making it unclear which entities S2K is supposed to be marketing to under the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: S2K agrees, subject to the provisions of this Agreement, through its Authorized S2K Representatives, to use its reasonable best efforts to market the Funds to the Intermediaries, and to identify, refer and/or introduce Intermediaries to the Funds. In connection therewith, S2K may (i) engage in seminars, conferences and media interviews for financial intermediaries; (ii) distribute sales literature and other communications (including electronic media) regarding the Funds, subject to review and approval of such material by the Distributor; and (iii) perform other services reasonably contemplated in writing by S2K and the Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a restrictive definition of 'Intermediary', limiting it to broker-dealers registered with the SEC and FINRA. This directly contradicts the original definition within the same section, which included both broker-dealers and registered investment advisers. The contradicted text states that S2K will market to 'Intermediaries'. The new definition creates a contradiction because it is now unclear whether S2K is obligated to market to registered investment advisers, as the definition of Intermediaries has been altered to exclude them. This ambiguity creates an in-text contradiction, as per the definition provided of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The amount of compensation payable by the Distributor to S2K hereunder shall be determined on a class by class basis. At the direction of each Fund, ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K in the amounts set forth in Exhibit B hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: The amount of compensation payable by the Distributor to S2K hereunder shall be a fixed annual sum of $100,000, irrespective of the class of shares sold. At the direction of each Fund, ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K in the amounts set forth in Exhibit B hereto.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in how S2K is compensated. Initially, compensation is determined on a 'class by class basis' and linked to Exhibit B, which presumably outlines variable fees. By inserting 'The amount of compensation payable by the Distributor to S2K hereunder shall be a fixed annual sum of $100,000, irrespective of the class of shares sold,' the compensation becomes a fixed amount, directly contradicting the class-based system previously described and creating an internal contradiction regarding the dealer reallowance payments set forth in Exhibit B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fee Schedule At the direction of each Fund set forth in Exhibit A to this Agreement, ALPS or its designated agent will facilitate the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K (as a percentage of the offering price) in the amounts set forth in each such Fund's then-current Prospectus. Note: The following applies to all Funds set forth in Exhibit A to this Agreement with the exception of the NorthStar/Townsend Institutional Real Estate Fund Inc.: In no event will a Fund's aggregate selling commissions, dealer manager fees and distribution and servicing fees, if applicable, exceed 8.0% of the aggregate gross proceeds raised in the Fund's offering. Therefore, the Distributor's facilitation of the dealer reallowance payments set forth in each Fund's then-current Prospectus shall cease with respect to the applicable Fund as of the date such 8.0% threshold has been reached with respect to such Fund.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that compensation was determined on a 'class by class basis' and linked to Exhibit B. The changed text establishes a 'fixed annual sum of $100,000, irrespective of the class of shares sold.' This directly contradicts the variable, class-based compensation system described in the original text. Furthermore, it creates a contradiction with the 'Fee Schedule' which directs that ALPS will facilitate 'the payment of the applicable dealer reallowance fee to S2K (as a percentage of the offering price) in the amounts set forth in each such Fund's then-current Prospectus.' If S2K receives a fixed annual payment, then the percentage-based 'dealer reallowance fee' from the funds has no bearing on S2K compensation, thus creating an in-text contradiction that is of high importance.

perturbation.original_text: Each party to this Agreement shall obtain the other party's prior written consent before using any marketing or sales literature related to the consenting party, and shall not use the other party's names in any marketing or advertising materials without prior written consent from the consenting party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to this Agreement shall obtain the other party's prior written consent before using any marketing or sales literature related to the consenting party, and may use the other party's names in any marketing or advertising materials without prior written consent from the consenting party, provided such use is limited to identifying the parties involved in this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces conflicting statements regarding the use of names in marketing materials. The original text explicitly requires 'prior written consent' before using the other party's names. The added phrase 'and may use the other party's names in any marketing or advertising materials without prior written consent from the consenting party, provided such use is limited to identifying the parties involved in this agreement' allows use of names without prior consent under certain conditions. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether prior consent is always required or only sometimes required, leading to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party to this Agreement shall obtain the other party's prior written consent before using any marketing or sales literature related to the consenting party, and may use the other party's names in any marketing or advertising materials without prior written consent from the consenting party, provided such use is limited to identifying the parties involved in this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stipulates that 'prior written consent' is required for using the other party's name in marketing materials. The changed text then states that names 'may' be used without prior written consent under specific conditions. This directly contradicts the earlier requirement, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding when consent is needed, causing the text to be internally contradictory with the contradicted text.
